titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Russell Cheney Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 20, 2023 at 03:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English, with a small amount of material in French and German.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, ephemera, artwork, photographs, and computer media by or relating to American painter Russell Cheney.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 8 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 1-2: In-process material. Research use may require advance notice. For further information, consult Access Services.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: After they were photocopied, original materials in Boxes 1-2 were destroyed due to preservation concerns.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence and Other Papers, 1916-2018. II. Artwork, 1930s, undated. III. Photographs, 1881-1938, undated. IV. Computer Media, undated.

bioghist.head: Russell Cheney (1881-1945)
bioghist.p: Russell Cheney was an American painter known for his still lifes and landscapes. He graduated from Yale University, where he was a member of Skull and Bones, in 1904. From 1904 to 1907, he studied at the Art Students League in New York City, after which he attended the Académie Julian in Paris from 1907 to 1910. Cheney returned to New York and briefly served as president of the Art Students League from 1910 to 1911. In 1912, he established a studio in Manchester, Connecticut. Cheney traveled to Maine, Santa Fe, New Mexico, and California to paint. His work was exhibited at the Babcock Gallery in New York, the Wadsworth Athenaeum in Hartford, Connecticut, the 1933 Chicago World's Fair, the Montross Gallery in New York, the Addison Gallery of American Art at Phillips Academy in Andover, Massachusetts, and the Grace Horne Gallery in Boston. Early in his career, Cheney was diagnosed with tuberculosis and received treatment from 1916 to 1918 at the Colorado Springs sanatorium where his Yale friend, poet Phelps Putnam, was also a patient. In 1924, he met historian and literary critic F. O. Matthiessen (Yale 1923), and they began a relationship that lasted over 20 years.

Cheney died in his home in Kittery, Maine, on July 12, 1945. After his death, Matthiessen organized several exhibitions of Cheney's work at institutions in New England and New York and in 1947, he published the biography Russell Cheney 1881-1945: A Record of His Work .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Russell Cheney Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, ephemera, artwork, photographs, and computer media by or relating to American painter Russell Cheney. Correspondents include Malcolm Forbes, Penelope Hyde and Louis Hyde, Helen Bayne Knapp and Farwell Knapp, F. O. Matthiessen, Phelps Putnam, and Henry Varnum Poor. Photographs feature Cheney and his family and friends, as well as scenes from Southern California, Colorado, and New Mexico and images of his paintings. Artwork includes "Christmas cat cartoons," with pen and wash drawings by Cheney and words by Matthiessen. Also included are three poems by May Sarton, two of which are signed, most likely gifted to the Hydes.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Russell Cheney Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of photocopies of correspondence, a small amount of ephemera, and materials related to Cheney's death, memorials, and posthumous exhibits and publications. Included are letters from Russell Cheney to Helen Bayne Knapp and Louis Hyde; letters to Cheney from Malcolm Forbes, F. O. Matthiessen, Henry Varnum Poor, and Putnam Phelps; and third party correspondence of Helen Bayne and Farwell Knapp and Penelope and Louis Hyde. The series also contains three printed poems by May Sarton, one of which is signed and addressed to the Hydes.

did.unittitle: Cheney, Russell to Helen Bayne Knapp (including photographs)
Cheney, Russell to Louis Hyde (one letter from Hyde to Cheney)
Hyde, Penelope to Louis Hyde (with a few from Louis) [photocopies]
Knapp, Helen Bayne third party correspondence (primarily to Penelope and Louis Hyde) [photocopies]
Matthiessen, F. O. to Russell Cheney [photocopies]
Pelly, Bernard B. (including letter from F. O. Matthiessen) [photocopies]
Posthumous correspondence, clippings, lists, and other materials [photocopies]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of original artwork by Russell Cheney, including a group of "Christmas cat cartoons" he created with F. O. Matthieson, and an untitled painting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of photographs, photographic postcards, and photograph albums of Russell Cheney with family and friends including F. O. Matthiessen, Helen Bayne Knapp and Farwell Knapp, Phelps Putnam, and Malcolm Forbes. Also included are images of Cheney's artwork and photographs of mountains and other scenes in California, Colorado, and New Mexico.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Family and friends, California and Colorado landscapes
Family and friends ("miscellaneous photos from Betsy Packard")
Family and friends ("miscellaneous photos from Betsy Packard to Carol L. Cheney")
Family and friends ("photos from Betsy Packard to Carol L. Cheney")
Pueblo of Santa Clara, New Mexico, Tufelo Romero in Taos, and unidentified

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of one DVD related to Russell Cheney.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
